title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a local news quiz covering recent Greater Boston stories.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
The quiz aims to test readers' knowledge of trending Greater Boston events.

title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: Boston needle pickup requests rose 71% from 2022 to 2025 but remained 17% below 2020 levels.
Jamaica Plain saw a 149% increase in needle pickup requests from 235 in 2020 to 585 in 2025.
Back Bay recorded the highest increase, with requests jumping from 2 to over 100 at Blagden and Exeter streets by 2025.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo of Scituate, Mass. is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo is inspired by her mother's Alzheimer's and their shared memories, particularly related to cooking.
Sorting through her mother's kitchen items brings back vivid memories of her mother's care and nurturing.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are 34-22-5 and hold the second Wild Card spot in the Eastern Conference.
The Bruins' road record stands at 11-14-4 after a 6-3 loss to the Nashville Predators.
General Manager Don Sweeney is considering trade options but hesitant on short-term player rentals.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A U.S. Court of Appeals judge granted Seamus Culleton a stay of deportation pending review, but he remains in detention.
Culleton overstayed a 90-day visa in 2009 and was arrested in Saugus, Massachusetts by ICE agents in 2025.
Culleton, married to a U.S. citizen, has a pending green card application and claims poor conditions at the Texas detention camp.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in a house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough, Tuesday morning.
Foxborough Fire Department extinguished the fire with help from Mansfield and Plainville departments; the home suffered serious damage.
A town police officer initially detected the fire; the cause is under investigation by officials.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded by 151 acres between 2019 and 2024, reaching 28.5% citywide coverage.
Growth in public parks and rights-of-way contributed to the increase, reversing the previous five-year period's static coverage.
East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury saw the largest net gains in tree canopy.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group committed to contributing to the $7.8 million security cost for World Cup events in Foxborough.
Foxborough officials remain unsatisfied, citing concerns about the adequacy of the current financial arrangements for security costs.
Foxborough Select Board will decide on March 17 whether to grant an event license for the World Cup at Gillette Stadium.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern, a bar popular with Northeastern students, is closing after nearly 10 years on Gainsborough Street, Fenway.
Rising insurance costs are forcing the closure; liability insurance quotes jumped from $18,000 to $75,000-$82,000 annually.
Managing partner Chuck Hitchcock cites shifts in social habits and the broken insurance system as challenges facing the business.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to return for the Celtics on Friday against the Dallas Mavericks after recovering from a ruptured Achilles.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla may adjust rotations and Tatum is likely to be on a minutes restriction below 30 minutes.
Tatum's return may affect roster positions and player minutes, particularly impacting Baylor Scheierman and other young guards.
